messages.text:
So order of things, did Chris talk to Jim and suggest that a spot on our board is potentially available or did you and he talk first? Because if it is the former then I think that's a problem, and to a lesser extent if it's the latter it's still an issue. We agreed that people weren't going to go off and do major things without going through the proper channels. We have gotten ourselves into trouble with stuff like this in the past. We agreed that we needed to put policies and procedures in place for things like engaging potential new stakeholders in place. We need accountability. That's what we agreed to. If you think I'm interpreting this the wrong way, tell me and preferably here so that I have the opportunity to digest it a bit.

I'm worried that we're going to trade one group making unilateral decisions for another. And this isn't an anti-Chris. I have worked my ass off to be collegial with Chris. I've taken his calls, I've been respectful and I've given credit where credit is due. But all I did at lunch was question the process for how Jim Turner came up, and I was attacked for it. You said I was "prickly". I wasn't "prickly". I challenged the method of how something was done and then I was attacked, and then I became defensive.

I had walked away from SoyLei for more or less a similar reason. I challenged Chris and others when they didn't follow the process, and then I was basically told to step aside. So I did. I came back into it because I was told that the company needed help, and I've been pouring myself into it ever since. Everything I've tried to write after this is starting to feel uncoordinated, whiney and more of a rant so I'm going to stop here and let you respond

Acknowledging that I've read this. I will try to respond later

I'm not really in a wall of text frame of mind though so we can talk out whatever I don't address tomorrow morning

I'm sorry I just don't have the mental energy to try to craft the appropriately nuanced rebuttal. 

My short answer is no one is making unilateral decisions, or bilateral ones for that matter.

The not having the mental energy is fair. It's been an exhausting couple of weeks. Here is my thought to leave you with, and I ask that you seriously think about it. If Nacu or Marv had done the same thing, would you have been ok with it? Do you think Chris would have been ok with it?

And I'm genuinely worried that if we were to bring someone like a Jim into be CEO or other primary leadership position in, I believe that they also would probably have issue with one of the members going off and talking about serious issues like that. If we can't even have a calm discussion about how this was handled then how are we going to respond to someone like that?

I tried to make it as short of a rant as I can. No response expected or requested tonight, we can discuss more tomorrow or whenever.
